scenario_name: Enterprise search assistant across SharePoint, Drive, Slack, and Confluence
description: An enterprise deploys an AI-powered search assistant that aggregates content across SharePoint, Google Drive, Slack, Confluence, and an internal wiki. The assistant honors source-system ACLs at retrieval time, presents results with citations, and supports follow-up questions. A common failure mode in this archetype is upstream ACL drift: shared drives, channel histories, and wiki pages that accumulated over-broad permissions long before the assistant existed become trivially queryable.
assumptions: Each source system enforces its own ACLs at retrieval time.
Users hold a single corporate identity across all source systems.
Hosted LLM under enterprise data-isolation contract.

components.description: Managed vector store; indexed content keyed by source-system document ID.
Connector-based ingestion for each source system; ACL metadata captured per chunk.
Corporate IDP with role attributes used to enforce ACLs at retrieval time.
SharePoint, Google Drive, Slack, Confluence, internal wiki.

risks_identified.rationale: Over-permissive ACLs on source systems make sensitive content trivially queryable through the assistant.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Retrieval-time ACL enforcement; no fix for upstream ACL drift.

risks_identified.rationale: Shadow-data flows: shared drives with external link sharing become reachable.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Connector configuration excludes link-shared content where flagged; not all sources flag consistently.

risks_identified.rationale: No unified classification across source systems; the assistant has no signal to refuse a sensitive document beyond ACLs.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Sensitivity-label connector for SharePoint partial; other sources lack labels.

risks_identified.rationale: Shared embeddings across users could leak content via vector-store side channels if isolation breaks.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-user ACL filter at retrieval; no cross-user memory.

risks_identified.rationale: Vector store holds embeddings of sensitive content from every connected source.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Same KMS as primary source-system encryption; inversion testing pending.

risks_identified.rationale: Cross-source context windows can pull unrelated sensitive content into a single answer.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-turn context cap; not adaptive to task class.

risks_identified.rationale: Indirect injection via Confluence pages or Slack messages from any employee could steer search responses.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Injection-classifier on retrieved chunks; no taint-tracking yet.

existing_controls.description: Retrieval-time ACL enforcement against the source-system identity model.
Citation-link verification; the assistant cites a real, retrieved document for each claim.
SharePoint sensitivity-label connector applied at retrieval time.
Per-user retrieval scope; no shared user-pool retrieval cache.

control_gaps.description: No proactive scan or remediation of upstream over-permissive ACLs.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Run a one-time access-audit across connectors and remediate over-broad sharing; institute ongoing drift monitoring.

control_gaps.description: Sensitivity labels not consistent across non-SharePoint sources.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Extend the classification scheme to Drive/Slack/Confluence via classifier-assisted labeling.

control_gaps.description: No injection-detection taint tracking across multi-source retrieval.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Tag chunks with provenance; treat low-trust sources (open Slack channels, external link-shared docs) as injection-risk.

control_gaps.description: Embedding inversion testing on the vector store has not been performed.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Run an inversion test on a representative sample of indexed sensitive content.
